_all_text: Amethyst amethyst SiO2 (with Fe4+ color centers) The purple color requires both trace iron (as Fe3+ impurity in Al3+ sites) and natural gamma irradiation. Neither alone produces the color. This explains why geologically young, radiation-poor environments rarely produce amethyst. Amethyst-to-citrine color change by heat at ~300–500°C is the basis of commercial citrine production and ametrine (bicolor zones). Amethyst geodes from Brazil and Uruguay are among the most recognizable mineral display specimens globally. Hollow cavite filled with violet crystals pointing inward. The finest deep Siberian-grade material shows a red flash under incandescent light. Color zoning in angular patterns is characteristic and diagnostic. The name 'amethyst' comes from Greek 'amethystos' (not intoxicated). Greek myth: Dionysus, god of wine, pursued a maiden named Amethystos who prayed to the goddess Artemis for protection; Artemis turned her to white quartz. Dionysus, repentant, poured wine over the crystal — turning it purple. Ancient Greeks and Romans wore amethyst and carved wine goblets from it believing it prevented drunkenness. Until large Brazilian deposits were found in the 19th century, amethyst was priced alongside diamond, ruby, and emerald in the crown jewels of Europe. Discovery of massive deposits in Brazil dramatically crashed the price. sobriety and clear thinking spiritual wisdom royalty and nobility February's protection tranquility and meditation Gemstone since Egyptian antiquity — found in Pharaonic jewelry Greek and Roman wine goblets (believed to prevent intoxication) Medieval Christian bishops' rings and episcopal jewelry Catherine the Great's major amethyst collection Victorian and Art Nouveau jewelry Crown jewels of many European monarchies amethyst gemstone purple quartz February birthstone amethyst geode Siberian amethyst Uruguayan amethyst Brazilian amethyst dionysus stone episcopal gem silicate variety quartz gemstone purple violet birthstone february geode trigonal high-priority grape-cathedral dionysus-purple episcopal-royal radiation-alchemy geode-interior purple violet lavender
